subject: Computer Networking
title: The Telegraph: First Network
description: A quick win: understand The Telegraph: First Network.

hook.text: In 1844, Morse tapped a message from Washington to Baltimore. Instant long-distance communication was born.

buildup.text: The telegraph used electrical signals over copper wire. Operators encoded letters as dots and dashes.

discovery.text: By 1866, a transatlantic cable connected Europe and America. A message that once took weeks arrived in minutes.

twist.text: The telegraph network had routing, relay stations, and standard codes — the same concepts modern networks use.

climax.text: It transformed business, journalism, and war. Decisions that took weeks could now be made in hours.

punchline.text: The telegraph was the internet of the 1800s.

quiz.question: What year was the first successful telegraph message sent?
